metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_2918
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Fig.2: "Ultrastructural localization of calcyon in the macaque prefrontal cortex"
- "vesicular intracellular organelles indicative of the endocytic and/or recycling machinery (28, 29) were labeled in extrasynaptic and intracellular regions of spines (Fig. 2B), dendrites (Fig. 2D), and axon terminals (Fig. 2C).The association of calcyon immunoreactivity to the intracellular side of the plasma membrane (Fig. 2, B-D) and the absence of labeling of the multivesicular bodies (MVB; Fig. 2, B and D) and of the synaptic membrane specialization of axon terminals and dendrites (Fig. 2, B-D) further support the observation that calcyon specifically localizes to intracellular endocytic pathways within neurons of the primate prefrontal cortex. Collectively, the above EM data highlight the potential for calcyon to regulate clathrin-mediated vesicle trafficking in neurons at several points along the endocytic and late secretory pathways (30).". Experimental description: - "Electron Microscopy-Animals used for immunohistochemistry were housed and treated according to federal and institutional guidelines. Two adult rhesus monkeys (Macaca mulatta) were transcardially..."
